steps.title: Break the charge into two pieces
steps.content: The first $30$ people cost 26 dollars each, a fixed amount of $30\cdot 26=780$ dollars. Anyone beyond that is charged 18 dollars each, and there are $n-30$ such people when $n\ge 30$.

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• A) $18n+240$: gives 780 dollars at $n=30$ and adds 18 dollars per extra person. Matches.
  • B) $18n+26$: at $n=30$ this is 566 dollars, far less than 780 dollars. Eliminated.
  • C) $18n+780$: at $n=30$ this is 1,320 dollars, overcharging the base group. Eliminated.
  • D) $44n-540$: at $n=30$ this is 780 dollars (coincidence), but slope is 44 dollars per person, not 18 dollars. Eliminated.
A is correct.
